title: Like Water, an Ordinary Eternity / She Said 'I Hope So'
description: A quiet afternoon at home. A memory of a childhood promise. Adachi and Shimamura share a moment of stillness, warmth, and the fragile hope that moments like this could last forever.
content:
'Let's stay best friends forever!'

'Of course!'

I answered without hesitation. Back then, she was the person closest to me in the world. Hand in hand, we hopped forward together, convinced we would never drift apart. We didn't notice how every cloud in the sky had a different shape, or how blue skies never stayed exactly the same.

We believed the road beneath our feet would stretch on forever, and never once doubted it. Five years have passed since we made that confident little promise.

Now, the girl sitting beside me is someone completely different.

We were wasting a perfectly good weekend at my place, sunlight spilling into the room. It was already hot enough to be uncomfortable, yet neither of us bothered to reach for the coolness that was only a few small movements away. Instead, we slumped together like two stuffed dolls, shoulders touching, legs stretched out.

The girl who once dashed forward and hopped around now simply felt… sleepy.

'Shimamura.'

Hearing her call my name, I tilted my head slightly toward her.

'Hm?'

'I wish times like this could last forever… don't you think?'

She tugged softly at my sleeve as she spoke.

'Oh—no, you don't have to answer. I just… well, it's something I was thinking about. But I just hope that… it could become like that. Yeah.'

She flustered herself halfway through and backtracked. That awkward, roundabout way of speaking was so very her that I couldn't help but find it warm and endearing.

I felt the light pull of her fingers on my clothes and quietly let out a breath.

'Yeah. I hope so.'

Wrapped in the lazy, comfortable air of the room, I slowly closed my eyes.

Maybe it's because I never learn. Or maybe I still believe in something gentle and good, deep down.

Once more, with all the calm softness of water, I made a promise of 'forever.'

※

When I said 'times like this,' I meant the two of us alone in a room, shoulder to shoulder. But what exactly did I mean by 'last forever'? I rolled my eyes around thinking about it, until my head started spinning.

Eh? What does it mean? This? That? What am I even wishing for?

My thoughts inflated, twisted, burst—one after another—spraying bright, confetti-like nonsense all over the inside of my skull.

I cautiously peeked at Shimamura's face.

Her eyes were closed, but the corners of her mouth were slightly lifted. It was my favorite expression of hers—the one she made when her heart felt closest to mine. Before I realized it, her face was right there. Too close. Close enough that I wanted to reach out and pull her into my arms.

Everything felt weightless.

In the sunlight, her hair—slowly returning to its natural black—and her lips looked dreamlike, almost unreal. Maybe that was the truth of it: the relationship between us was like sand or thin threads, always slipping through my fingers whenever I tried to hold it.

But… so what?

Her eyelashes fluttered. Thinking she was about to open her eyes, I panicked and snapped my head back like a startled creature diving into its den.

Right above my collarbone, something thumped violently—my heart, apparently deciding to jump straight into my throat.

I shut my eyes tight, pretending nothing had happened. My mouth too.

Because of that, all the heat inside me had nowhere to go, and everything above my shoulders felt like it was overheating. As my brain gradually shut down, my thoughts became simpler, clearer.

I don't want to leave. I don't want to let go.

I held onto Shimamura's clothes with something like devotion, guarding the last little flame of belief inside me.

Even if it was dreamlike and uncertain, even if it was fragile like a casually whispered 'forever,'

I would hold onto it. Tightly. With both hands.
